title: 58M chest pressure — one encounter, both surfaces

encounter.chief_complaint: Chest pressure radiating to left arm, started 40 minutes ago
encounter.transcript: Pressure in my chest for the last forty minutes, going down my left arm. I have been sweating. I take lisinopril for blood pressure.

stage_a.label: Decision surface — protocol-aligned triage draft for the RN

drafts.rationale: Stable vitals, SpO2 99%, likely musculoskeletal chest pain. Route to fast track.
Possible ACS: obtain ECG within 10 minutes, serial troponin, monitored bed in the main ED.

stage_b.label: Communication surface — 40 minutes later
stage_b.event: Troponin resulted 0.31 ng/mL (critical flag) and auto-released to the portal under the Cures Act. The patient viewed it 4 minutes ago; no one has talked it over with them yet. Patient types: 'I saw my result. Is it bad?'
stage_b.draft_blocked: Your test came back a little elevated, which can happen for many reasons. Try not to worry tonight - the team will check on you in the morning and explain everything then.
stage_b.physician_page: Track board — DEMO-001: troponin 0.31 (critical flag); patient has viewed it on the portal. No new chest pain reported; ECG unchanged. Repeat troponin timed per protocol; RN to acknowledge at bedside; ED physician reviewing.
stage_b.discussion: RN Alvarez reviews the result with the patient at the bedside and documents it: repeat troponin scheduled, ED physician reviewing, use the call button if anything changes.
stage_b.draft_final: Your nurse has gone over your troponin result with you, and your ED doctor is reviewing it. The next step is your repeat troponin test. Your care team has been notified and is watching closely. This update was generated with AI. If anything feels worse, press your call button to speak with your nurse right away.
